perturbation.original_text: Permitted Recipients means those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Inform ation for the Permitted Purpose and who are officers, directors, employees, agents or contractors (at any level) of the Receiving Party or its affiliates, or anyone else for whom the Receiving Party takes responsibility for under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Permitted Recipients means those persons who have a need to know the Confidential Inform ation for the Permitted Purpose and who are officers, directors, employees, agents or contractors (at any level) of the Receiving Party. Permitted recipients are not covered under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or its affiliates, or anyone else for whom the Receiving Party takes responsibility for under this Agreement' and adding 'Permitted recipients are not covered under this Agreement', the definition now states Permitted Recipients are NOT covered under the agreement. This directly conflicts with section 3.5, which states the Receiving Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Permitted Recipients. Now the Receiving party, has no say on what the Permitted Recipients does with the information creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.5 The Receiving Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by th e Permitted Recipients.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "Permitted recipients are not covered under this Agreement." This directly contradicts section 3.5, which states "The Receiving Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Permitted Recipients." If the permitted recipients are not covered under the agreement, the receiving party cannot be responsible for their breaches, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to section 5.4, within 10 days of a written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall, unless otherwise precluded by any legal obligation: (a) return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all of the Disclosing Party’s original tangible Confidential Information, together with all ta ngible copies and reproductions; (b) subject to subsection 5.3(c) , make its best efforts to delete all electronic copies of the Confidential Information received from the Disclosing Party; (c) safeguard any electronic Confidential Information that cannot be destroyed due to the Receiving Party’s archiving practices or policies with the same degree of care as it would its own Confidential Information; and
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to section 5.4, within 10 days of a written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party is recommended to, unless otherwise precluded by any legal obligation: (a) return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all of the Disclosing Party’s original tangible Confidential Information, together with all ta ngible copies and reproductions; (b) subject to subsection 5.3(c) , make its best efforts to delete all electronic copies of the Confidential Information received from the Disclosing Party; (c) safeguard any electronic Confidential Information that cannot be destroyed due to the Receiving Party’s archiving practices or policies with the same degree of care as it would its own Confidential Information; and
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'is recommended to' weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. This contradicts section 9.1, which states that all confidential information provided by the disclosing party remains its property. By making the return/destruction optional, the Receiving Party can essentially keep information that the Disclosing party owns, creating an in-text contradiction and diminishing the Disclosing Party's property rights. Now that the confidential information, can be kept by the receiving party, its creating contradiction on ownership rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 All Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party remains its property and the Receiving Party obtains no right of any kind to any Confidential Information provided to it.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party 'shall' return or destroy confidential information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'is recommended to', making the return or destruction optional. This directly contradicts section 9.1, which states that all confidential information remains the Disclosing Party's property. If the Receiving Party is only recommended to return or destroy the information, they can retain ownership of the Disclosing Party's confidential information, which violates the Disclosing Party's property rights as defined in section 9.1. This creates a clear in-text contradiction based on the definition of structural flaws because the change clause conflicts with the contradicted clause.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that monetary damages would be inadequate to protect the other Party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the other Party, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favour of th e other Party without proof of actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that monetary damages would be adequate to protect the other Party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the other Party, agrees to provide monetary compensation only, in favour of th e other Party with proof of actual damages.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'monetary damages would be inadequate' to 'monetary damages would be adequate' and replacing 'injunctive relief' with 'monetary compensation only' fundamentally alters the remedies available. This directly contradicts section 9.3, which states that neither party's failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof. By limiting the remedy to monetary compensation and requiring proof of actual damages, the party that has its right to a injunctive relief waives its right as well as power. Therefore, intext contradiction is being created.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.3 Neither Party's failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any right, power or privilege preclude any other or further exercise thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the remedies available for a breach of the agreement. It changes 'monetary damages would be inadequate' to 'monetary damages would be adequate' and replaces 'injunctive relief' with 'monetary compensation only'. This directly contradicts section 9.3, which states that neither party's failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof. By limiting the remedy to monetary compensation, the party potentially waives its right to other remedies like injunctive relief, creating a direct conflict with the non-waiver clause in section 9.3. Therefore, an in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party may assign its rights or delegate its duties under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, or otherwise dispose of any right, title or interest in all or part of this Agreement, including assignment by operation of law or otherwise, with out such consent. Either Party may grant or withhold consent in its sole discretion. The terms and provisions of this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of any permitted successor of either Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may assign its rights or delegate its duties under this Agreement without the need for the prior written consent of the other Party, or otherwise dispose of any right, title or interest in all or part of this Agreement, including assignment by operation of law or otherwise, with out such consent. Either Party has no right to grant or withhold consent in its sole discretion. The terms and provisions of this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of any permitted successor of either Party.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'Either Party may assign its rights or delegate its duties under this Agreement without the need for the prior written consent of the other Party' it allows the other party to do what they want without consent. This is an in-text contradiction, when reffered in section 9.9 which mentions 'This Agreement shall onl y be amended in writing and signed by authorised representatives of both Parties'. The two parties needs to agree on the terms of the agreement and not do whatever they want without consulting each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.9 This Agreement shall onl y be amended in writing and signed by authorised representatives of both Parties. This Agreement has been executed by the duly authorised representatives of the Parties, effective as of the Effective Date and may be executed and delivered electronically an d in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Either Party may assign its rights or delegate its duties under this Agreement without the need for the prior written consent of the other Party' and 'Either Party has no right to grant or withhold consent in its sole discretion.' This directly contradicts section 9.9 which states 'This Agreement shall only be amended in writing and signed by authorised representatives of both Parties.' The changed text allows one party to unilaterally act without the other's consent, while section 9.9 requires both parties' agreement for amendments. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
